L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1011
Which is the correct point for E when reflected in the line x=-1 ? (Diagram: A set of axes: x-axis from -5 to 5, y-axis from -5 to 3. Four points are plotted and joined to make a rectangle: (1,1), (3, 1), (3, 3) and (1,3). Point (3, 3) is labelled with the letter "E".)
  • A. (-3, 3)
  • B. (-5, 3)
  • C. (3, -3)
  • D. (3, -5)
Explanation: The correct answer is (-5, 3). A common mistake here: Reflects in an axis, instead of the given mirror line. Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a vertical line with the line of reflection given as an equation, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Reflects in an axis, instead of the given mirror line
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1058
A point, Q , has coordinates (-3,3) The point is reflected in the line y=-x What are the new coordinates of point Q ?
  • A. (3,3)
  • B. (-3,-3)
  • C. (-3,3)
  • D. (3,-3)
Explanation: The correct answer is (-3,3). A common mistake here: Believes reflection in y = -x changes sign of x coordinate only. Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a diagonal line with the line of reflection given as an equation, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes reflection in y = -x changes sign of x coordinate only
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1168
(Diagram: A coordinate grid with point V marked at (2,2) and point W marked at (-3,-1)) Describe the movements to get from point V to point W .
  • A. Left 4 squares, down 3 squares
  • B. Left 5 squares, down 3 squares
  • C. Right 5 squares, up 3 squares
  • D. Left 5 squares, up 3 squares
Explanation: The correct answer is Left 5 squares, down 3 squares. A common mistake here: When describing a translation, miscounts when crossing an axis. Double-check each step in describe simple directions to translate a shape or object using words not vectors to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: When describing a translation, miscounts when crossing an axis
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1210
(Diagram: A coordinate grid with a line segment labelled K from (-1,2) to (4,3) and a line segment marked L from (-3,-4) to (-2,0)) What can you say about line segments K and L ?
  • A. K is longer than L
  • B. K is shorter than L
  • C. K and L are the same length
  • D. We don't have enough information without measuring them
Explanation: The correct answer is K is longer than L. A common mistake here: Estimates the length of diagonal lines rather than working out based on horizontal and vertical measurements. Double-check each step in compare the lengths of diagonal line segments without calculating the exact length to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Estimates the length of diagonal lines rather than working out based on horizontal and...
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1234
(Diagram: A pair of axes: x-axis is from -3 to 4; y-axis is from -4 to 4. A quadrilateral, labelled "P", is made by joining the following points, in order, with straight lines: (-3, 1), (-1, 1), (-1, 3), (-3, 4) and back to (-3, 1). The point (-1, 3) is marked with a red dot. Also, the following points are plotted and labelled with the following letters: A (3, 1); B (1, -1); C (3, -1); D (1, -3).) Shape P is rotated 180^° through the origin. Where will the red point be on the rotated shape?
  • A. A
  • B. B
  • C. C
  • D. D
Explanation: The correct answer is D. A common mistake here: When asked to rotate by 180 degrees rotates by 90 degrees. Double-check each step in rotate an object or point 180° about a point, where the point lies on the edge or outside of the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: When asked to rotate by 180 degrees rotates by 90 degrees
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1274
Which is the correct description of the transformation that maps shape A onto shape B ? (Diagram: A coordinate grid with two triangles drawn on it. Triangle A has the coordinates: (6,4) (4,4) and (4,5). Triangle B has the coordinates: (-4,4) (-2,4) and (-2,5).)
  • A. Reflection in y -axis
  • B. Reflection in x -axis
  • C. Reflection in y=1
  • D. Reflection in x=1
Explanation: The correct answer is Reflection in x=1. A common mistake here: Reflects in an axis, instead of the given mirror line. Double-check each step in describe a reflection in a vertical line by giving the line of reflection as an equation, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Reflects in an axis, instead of the given mirror line
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1304
The triangle is reflected in the dashed line. What are the new coordinates of point P ? (Diagram: A set of axes: x-axis from -1 to 7, y-axis from -2 to 8. Three points are plotted and joined to make a triangle: (2, 4), (4, 4), (2, 7). Point (2, 4) is labelled with the letter "P". The line y=4 is shown with a blue dashed line.)
  • A. (2,3)
  • B. (2,4)
  • C. (2,2)
  • D. (2,-4)
Explanation: The correct answer is (2,4). A common mistake here: Does not understand that a point on the line of reflection will stay the same when reflected . Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a horizontal line with the line of reflection drawn, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not understand that a point on the line of reflection will stay the same when refl...
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1342
A point, Q , has coordinates (4,-4) The point is reflected in the line y=-x What are the new coordinates of point Q?
  • A. (4.4)
  • B. (-4,-4)
  • C. (-4,4)
  • D. (4,-4)
Explanation: The correct answer is (4,-4). A common mistake here: Does not understand that a point on the line of reflection will stay the same when reflected . Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a diagonal line with the line of reflection given as an equation, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not understand that a point on the line of reflection will stay the same when refl...
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1442
The triangle is reflected in the x -axis What are the new coordinates of point P ? (Diagram: A set of axes: x-axis from -1 to 8, y-axis from -5 to 6. Three points are plotted and joined to make a triangle: (2, 0), (4, 0), (2, 3). Point (2, 3) is labelled with the letter "P".)
  • A. (2,3)
  • B. (2,2)
  • C. (3,2)
  • D. (2,-3)
Explanation: The correct answer is (2,-3). A common mistake here: Believes that when a shape touches the mirror line, all of its vertices will stay the same when reflected. Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a horizontal line with the line of reflection given as an equation, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes that when a shape touches the mirror line, all of its vertices will stay the s...
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1557
Which is the correct point for E when reflected in the line x=0 ? (Diagram: A 4 quadrant, coordinate grid with a triangle drawn on it with the coordinates (-4,1) (-1,1) and (-2,3). E is the point (-2,3))
  • A. A is the coordinate (2,3)
  • B. B is the coordinate (-2,-3)
  • C. C is the coordinate (2,-3)
  • D. None of these
Explanation: The correct answer is A is the coordinate (2,3). A common mistake here: Thinks x = 0 is the x axis. Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a vertical line with the line of reflection given as an equation, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Thinks x = 0 is the x axis
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1572
The triangle is reflected in the dashed line What are the new coordinates of point P ? (Diagram: A coordinate grid with the reflection line y=-x drawn and a triangle with the coordinates (-2,3) (-1,4) and (0,3). P is the point (0,3))
  • A. (-6,3)
  • B. (-3,6)
  • C. (-3,3)
  • D. (-3,0)
Explanation: The correct answer is (-3,0). A common mistake here: Counts the squares rather than the diagonals when reflecting in y=x or y=-x . Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a diagonal line with the line of reflection drawn, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Counts the squares rather than the diagonals when reflecting in y=x or y=-x
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1590
Which of the following describes how to get from point P to point Q ? (Diagram: Point P and Point Q are marked on a grid. Q is 4 squares to the right and 2 squares above P)
  • A. 4 squares right 2 squares up
  • B. 5 squares right 3 squares up
  • C. 4 squares left 2 squares down
  • D. 5 squares right 2 squares up
Explanation: The correct answer is 4 squares right 2 squares up. A common mistake here: When describing a translation, counts the corners of squares (including the starting one) rather than the number of jumps between squares. Double-check each step in describe simple directions to translate a shape or object using words not vectors to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: When describing a translation, counts the corners of squares (including the starting on...
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1653
(Diagram: A pair of axes: x-axis is from -2 to 4; y-axis is from -2 to 4. A 6-sided polygon, labelled "P", is made by joining the following points, in order, with straight lines: (1, 2), (2, 2), (2, 3), (4, 3), (4, 4), (1, 4) and back to (1, 2). The point (2, 2) is marked with a red dot. Also, the following points are plotted and labelled with the following letters: A (-2, -2); B (2, -2); C (-1, -2); D (-2, -1).) Shape P is rotated 180^° through the origin. Where will the red point be on the rotated shape?
  • A. A
  • B. B
  • C. C
  • D. D
Explanation: The correct answer is A. A common mistake here: Reflects instead of rotates. Double-check each step in rotate an object or point 180° about a point, where the point lies on the edge or outside of the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Reflects instead of rotates
L2 eedi diagram eedi_1695
Tom and Katie are discussing reflections (Diagram: Tom says this reflection will create a rectangle. There is a diagonal dashed line with a square sitting on the line.) (Diagram: Katie says this reflection will create a square. There is a diagonal dashed line with a rectangle sitting on the line.) Who do you agree with?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Both Tom and Katie. A common mistake here: Cannot reflect shape when line of symmetry is diagonal . Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a diagonal line with the line of reflection drawn, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Cannot reflect shape when line of symmetry is diagonal
L2 eedi diagram eedi_1705
Shape A has been enlarged to get shape B. What is the scale factor of the enlargement? (Diagram: Triangle A is two squares across one square up. Triangle B is six squares across and three squares up.)
  • A. 1/3
  • B. 3
  • C. -3
  • D. 9
Explanation: The correct answer is 3. A common mistake here: Divides length of original by length of image rather finding length of image divided by length of original to find an enlargement scale factor. Double-check each step in describe an enlargement, with no centre of enlargement given, by a positive scale factor bigger than 1 to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Divides length of original by length of image rather finding length of image divided by...
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1748
Which line gives the reflection of shape P onto shape Q? (Diagram: A coordinate grid with two right angled triangles drawn, labelled P and Q. P has the coordinates: (2,5) (2,8) and (4,5). Q has the coordinates: (5,2) (8,2) and (5,4).)
  • A. y=x
  • B. y=4.5
  • C. y=-x
  • D. This is not a reflection
Explanation: The correct answer is y=x. A common mistake here: Believes lines of reflection must be vertical or horizontal. Double-check each step in describe a reflection in a diagonal line by giving the line of reflection as an equation, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes lines of reflection must be vertical or horizontal
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1762
How would you describe the movement to get from the star to the triangle? (Diagram: A set of axes: x-axis from 0 to 5, y-axis from 0 to 5. A purple triangle symbol is at point (2,1) and a yellow star symbol is at point (1, 4).)
  • A. 1 square left, 3 squares up
  • B. 1 square down, 3 squares right
  • C. 1 squares right, 4 squares down
  • D. 1 squares right, 3 squares down
Explanation: The correct answer is 1 squares right, 3 squares down. A common mistake here: When describing a translation, goes from the image to the original. Double-check each step in describe simple directions to translate a shape or object using words not vectors to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: When describing a translation, goes from the image to the original
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1798
The triangle is reflected in the dashed line. What are the new coordinates of point P ? (Diagram: A coordinate grid with a reflection line drawn and a triangle. The reflection line passes through the coordinates: (-1,-1) (0,0) (1,1) (2,2) etc. The triangle has coordinates: (2,2) (3,3) and (4,2). P is the coordinate (4,2).)
  • A. (2,2)
  • B. (2,0)
  • C. (2,4)
  • D. (0,2)
Explanation: The correct answer is (2,4). A common mistake here: Fails to reflect across mirror line. Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a diagonal line with the line of reflection drawn, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Fails to reflect across mirror line

(Diagram: A grid with a left-leaning parallelogram drawn in the upper half and a blue line across the middle, marked 'line of reflection'.) (Diagram: Tom does this: an image of a grid with a left-leaning parallelogram drawn in the upper half, a blue line across the middle and a left-leaning parallelogram drawn immediately below.) (Diagram: Katie does this: An image of a grid with a left-leaning parallelogram drawn in the upper half, a blue line across the middle and a left-leaning parallelogram drawn one line down from the blue line.)
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Neither is correct. A common mistake here: Fails to reflect across mirror line. Double-check each step in reflect an object or a point in a horizontal line with the line of reflection drawn, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Fails to reflect across mirror line

The triangle is enlarged by scale factor 3 , with the centre of enlargement at (1,0) . What are the new coordinates of the point marked T ? (Diagram: A coordinate grid with the x-axis going from -1 to 10 and the y-axis going from -1 to 7. 3 points are plotted and joined with straight lines to form a triangle. The points are (1,1), (1,4) and (3,1). Point (3,1) is labelled as T. Point (1,0) is also plotted.)
  • A. (7,3)
  • B. (1,3)
  • C. (6,4)
  • D. (9,3)
Explanation: The correct answer is (7,3). A common mistake here: Added rather than multiplied by a scale factor. Double-check each step in enlarge a shape, with a centre of enlargement given, by a positive scale factor bigger than 1, where the centre of enlargement lies on the edge or outside of the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Added rather than multiplied by a scale factor

(Diagram: A grid of 8 squares across and 7 squares up. A blue isosceles triangle, labelled "P", is drawn on the grid. Its corners are at points: 4 squares up from the bottom left corner of the grid; 2 squares right and 3 squares up from the bottom left corner of the grid; 2 squares right and 5 squares up from the bottom left corner of the grid. Another blue isosceles triangle (identical shape and size to P), labelled "Q", is also drawn on the grid. Its corners are at points: 6 squares right and 3 squares up from the bottom left corner of the grid; 6 squares right and 5 squares up from the bottom left corner of the grid; 8 squares right and 4 squares up from the bottom left corner of the grid. A light blue vertical line, labelled "A" goes from top to bottom of the grid, 2 squares to the right of the left-hand side of the grid. A dark blue vertical line, labelled "B" goes from top to bottom of the grid, 4 squares to the right of the left-hand side of the grid. A green vertical line, labelled "C" goes from top to bottom of the grid, 5 squares to the right of the left-hand side of the grid.) Which is the correct line of reflection to map triangle P to triangle Q ?
  • A. A
  • B. B
  • C. C
  • D. None of these lines are correct
Explanation: The correct answer is B. A common mistake here: Believes a line of reflection must be touching the original shape. Double-check each step in describe a reflection in a vertical line by drawing the line of reflection, where the line of reflection lies on the edge or outside the object to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes a line of reflection must be touching the original shape

Linear Equations

core · 179 questions · linear_equations
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t01_q03
If 5 x + 3 = 10 x – 17, what is the value of x ?
  • A. 1
  • B. 2
  • C. 3
  • D. 4
  • E. 5
Explanation: Step 1: Gather x terms on one side: 5x+3 = 10x−17 → subtract 5x both sides → 3 = 5x−17. Step 2: Isolate x: add 17 both sides → 20 = 5x. Step 3: Divide: x = 20÷5 = 4. Step 4: Verify: 5(4)+3=23 and 10(4)−17=23. ✓
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t03_q01
If pq + 12 = 3 p + pr, and q – r = 7, what is the value of p ?
  • A. –3
  • B. –4
  • C. 1/3
  • D. 3/5
  • E. –6/5
Explanation: Step 1: Move pr to the left, 3p to the right: pq – pr = 3p – 12. Step 2: Factor the left side by p: p(q – r) = 3p – 12. Step 3: Substitute q – r = 7: 7p = 3p – 12. Step 4: Solve: 4p = –12, so p = –3.
L1 act_master diagram act_math_t04_q01
In the following figure, the line passes through the origin and through P = (1, 3). If you were to draw a new line perpendicular to the first that also passes through P, what would be the equation of this new line?
  • A. y = 3x + 7/3
  • B. y = –3x – 1/3
  • C. y = 3x – 7/3
  • D. y = (1/3)x – 10/3
  • E. y = –(1/3)x + 10/3
Explanation: Step 1: The given line has slope 3. A perpendicular line has slope = –1/3 (negative reciprocal). Step 2: Reject choices F and H (slope +3, parallel not perpendicular) and G (slope –3, neither). Step 3: Between J and K: the line passes through a given point; plugging in to check the intercept confirms K.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t05_q03
If x = 6 and y = –2, what is the value of 3 xy + 2 x 2 – y 3 ?
  • A. 44
  • B. 48
  • C. 50
  • D. 52
  • E. 56
Explanation: x=6, y=–2. Evaluate 3xy + 2x² – y³. 3xy = 3(6)(–2) = –36. 2x² = 2(36) = 72. y³ = (–2)³ = –8. Expression: –36 + 72 – (–8) = –36 + 72 + 8 = 44.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t06_q02
What is the formula of a line that is perpendicular to and includes the point (3, 4)?
  • A. y = 3 x + 5
  • B. y = –3 x + 5
  • C. y = –3 x + 13
Explanation: Perpendicular line to one with slope 1/3 has slope –3 (negative reciprocal). Through (3,4): y–4 = –3(x–3) → y = –3x+9+4 = –3x+13.
L1 act_master diagram act_math_t06_q04
In the following figure, what is the midpoint of ?
  • A. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Recall midpoint formula: M = ((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2). Step 2: Identify the two endpoints U and V from the figure/problem. Step 3: Compute x-coordinate of midpoint: (x_U + x_V)/2. Step 4: Compute y-coordinate of midpoint: (y_U + y_V)/2. Step 5: The result is (−1, 1/2) = A.
L1 act_master diagram act_math_t07_q03
Which of the following points on the xy- graph is the x- intercept of the equation y = 2 x – 8?
  • A. (0, 4)
  • B. (0, )
  • C. (4, 0)
  • D. (–4, 0)
  • E. ( , 0)
Explanation: x-intercept: set y=0 in y=2x-8. 0=2x-8 → 2x=8 → x=4. Point: (4,0).
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t08_q01
If , what is the value of a ?
  • A. 0.01
  • B. 0.1
  • C. 1
  • D. 10
  • E. 100
Explanation: Step 1: The equation has the form a^(expr₁) = a^(expr₂) where expr₁ and expr₂ contain a variable. Step 2: For the equation to hold for all valid inputs, we need the base to make both sides equal regardless of exponent values. Step 3: a = 1 satisfies 1^(anything) = 1 on both sides. Step 4: No other positive value of a works for arbitrary exponents. Answer: H. 1.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t09_q05
At a banquet of 36 people, every person had a choice among beef stroganoff, chicken divan, and linguini primavera. If 25% chose beef stroganoff and 17 people chose chicken divan, how many people chose linguini primavera?
  • A. 7
  • B. 8
  • C. 9
  • D. 10
  • E. 11
Explanation: 36 total. Beef: 25% of 36 = 9. Chicken: 17. Linguini: 36-9-17=10.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t10_q05
What is the slope of the line 9 x – 3 y = 10?
  • A. 3
  • B. –3
  • C. 9
  • D. K.
Explanation: 9x–3y=10. Rearrange to slope-intercept: –3y=10–9x → y=3x–10/3. Slope=3.
L1 act_master diagram act_math_t11_q01
If the equation for the line shown in the following graph is , what is the value of kn ?
  • A. 9
  • B. 12
  • C. 15
  • D. 18
  • E. 24
Explanation: Step 1: The line equation is y = (1/3)x + 3. Step 2: Find k — the y-intercept (at x=0): y = 3, so k = 3. Step 3: Find n — the x-value where y=5: 5 = (1/3)n + 3 → (1/3)n = 2 → n = 6. Step 4: kn = 3 × 6 = 18. Answer: D. 18.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t11_q03
What is the equation for a line that intersects the origin and is perpendicular to 2 x – 4 y = 13?
  • A. y = –2
  • B. y = 2 x
  • C. y = –2 x
  • D. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Rewrite 2x − 4y = 13 in slope-intercept form: −4y = −2x + 13 → y = (1/2)x − 13/4. Slope = 1/2. Step 2: Perpendicular slope = −1/(1/2) = −2. Step 3: Line through origin (0, 0) with slope −2: y = −2x. Answer: C. y = −2x.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t11_q05
What is the value of x if ?
  • A. 17
  • B. –17
  • C. 29
  • D. –29
  • E. Cannot be determined from the information given.
Explanation: Step 1: Cross-multiply: 2x + 3y − 19 = 3(y + 5). Step 2: Expand right side: 3y + 15. Step 3: 2x + 3y − 19 = 3y + 15. Step 4: Subtract 3y from both sides: 2x − 19 = 15. Step 5: 2x = 34 → x = 17. Answer: A. 17.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t13_q02
The membership fees for WebFilms consist of a monthly charge of $14 and a one-time new-member fee of $16. Sherwood made a credit card payment of $100 to pay his WebFilms fees for a certain number of months, including the new-member fee. How many months of membership did Sherwood include in his credit card payment?
  • A. 4
  • B. 6
  • C. 7
  • D. 12
  • E. 14
Explanation: Step 1: Total = one-time fee + n × monthly fee. Step 2: 100 = 16 + 14n. Step 3: 84 = 14n. Step 4: n = 6 months → G.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t13_q03
If y = -6, what is the value of ?
  • A. -8
  • B. -4
  • C. 4
  • D. 9
  • E. 12
Explanation: Step 1: y=-6. Step 2: y²=(-6)²=36. Step 3: Numerator: y²-4=36-4=32. Step 4: Denominator: y-2=-6-2=-8. Step 5: 32/(-8)=-4 → B.
L1 act_master diagram act_math_t14_q02
Which of the following equations represents the linear relation shown in the standard ( x,y ) coordinate plane below?
  • A. y = -5 x
  • B. y = -6 x
  • C. y = -2 x + 2
  • D. y = -5 x + 6
  • E. y = -2 x + 6
Explanation: Step 1: From graph: y-intercept = 6. Step 2: Line is steep negative — x-intercept ≈ 1.2. Step 3: Slope = (0-6)/(1.2-0) = -5. Step 4: Equation: y=-5x+6 → D.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t14_q03
An integer, x , is subtracted from 6. That difference is then multiplied by 3. This product is 15 more than half the original integer. Which of the following equations represents this relationship?
  • A. 3(6 - x ) = + 15
  • B. 3(6 - x ) + 15 =
  • C. 3(6 - x ) = 15 -
  • D. x - 6 Ã_x0097_ 3 =
  • E. 6 + 3 = + 15
Explanation: Step 1: 'x is subtracted from 6' → (6-x). Step 2: 'Multiplied by 3' → 3(6-x). Step 3: 'Product is 15 more than half the original integer' → product = x/2 + 15. Step 4: Equation: 3(6-x) = x/2 + 15 → F.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t14_q04
The employees of two factories, X and Y, are comparing their respective production records. Factory X has already produced 18,000 units and can produce 120 units per day. Factory Y has produced only 14,500 units but can produce 155 units per day. If d represents the number of days (that is, days during which each factory is producing its maximum number of units), which of the following equations could be solved to determine the number of days until X's total production equals Y's total production?
  • A. 18,000 + 120 d = 14,500 + 155 d
  • B. 18,000 + 155 d = 14,500 + 120 d
  • C. (18,000 + 120) d = (14,500 + 155) d
  • D. (120 + 155) d = 18,000 - 14,500
  • E. (120 + 155) d = 18,000 + 14,500
Explanation: Step 1: Factory X total = 18,000 (current) + 120d (production at rate). Step 2: Factory Y total = 14,500 + 155d. Step 3: Set equal: 18,000+120d = 14,500+155d → A.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t15_q01
The kinetic energy, KE , of an object travelling at v velocity can be modeled by the equation KE = mv 2 , where m is the mass of the object. If an object is moving at a velocity of 9, and it has a kinetic energy of 120, about how great is the object's mass?
  • A. Between 0 and 1
  • B. Between 1 and 2
  • C. Between 2 and 3
  • D. 6
  • E. 13
Explanation: Step 1: KE=(1/2)mv². Substitute v=9, KE=120. Step 2: 120=(1/2)m(81)=40.5m. Step 3: m=120/40.5=240/81≈2.96. Step 4: 2<2.96<3 → C.
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t15_q02
Let x , y , and z be distinct positive integers. What is the fourth term of the geometric sequence below? 2 xz , 2 x 2 yz , 2 x 3 y 2 z ,â_x0080_¦
  • A. 2 x 2 yz
  • B. 2 x 4 y 3 z
  • C. 2 x 3 yz 2
  • D. 4 x 3 y 2 z
  • E. 4 x 4 y 3 z 2
Explanation: Step 1: Find common ratio r=T2/T1=(2x²yz)/(2xz)=xy. Step 2: Verify T3/T2=(2x³y²z)/(2x²yz)=xy ✓. Step 3: 4th term = T3 × r = 2x³y²z × xy = 2x⁴y³z → G.
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1028
What is the height of the rectangle as indicated by the arrow? (Diagram: A pair of axes with no labels. 4 points are marked and joined with straight lines to make a rectangle. The top left corner of the rectangle is labelled with co-ordinate (-4,7). The bottom right corner of the rectangle is labelled with co-ordinate (2,-12). A double-ended vertical arrow spans the height of the rectangle, starting level with (2,-12) and ending level with the corner of the rectangle directly above (2,-12).)
  • A. 5
  • B. 6
  • C. 2
  • D. 19
Explanation: The correct answer is 19. A common mistake here: Does not know how to find the length of a line segment from coordinates. Double-check each step in solve coordinate geometry questions involving quadrilaterals to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not know how to find the length of a line segment from coordinates
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1065
What is the equation of this line? (Diagram: The diagram shows a pair of axes, x and y ranging from -6 to 5 horizontally and -3 to 4 vertically. There is a horizontal line that passes through the y axis at -2.)
  • A. x=-2
  • B. y=-2 x
  • C. x y=-2
  • D. y=-2
Explanation: The correct answer is y=-2. A common mistake here: Confuses the equations of vertical and horizontal lines. Double-check each step in recognise the equations of horizontal lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Confuses the equations of vertical and horizontal lines
L1 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1096
What is the midpoint of (4,3) and (1,-5) ?
  • A. (2.5,-1)
  • B. (5,-2)
  • C. (2.5,-4)
  • D. Impossible as you can't have a decimal coordinate
Explanation: The correct answer is (2.5,-1). A common mistake here: Does not divide by 2 when finding midpoint. Double-check each step in calculate the midpoint between two coordinates in 2d, where all the values are integers but at least one is negative to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not divide by 2 when finding midpoint
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1176
(Diagram: A rectangle drawn on a coordinate grid. Three of the vertices are labelled (3,12) (10,12) and (10,7). There is a red arrow between (3,12) and (10,12) indicating the width.) What is the width of this rectangle as indicated by the arrow?
  • A. 12
  • B. 7
  • C. 5
  • D. 13
Explanation: The correct answer is 7. A common mistake here: Does not know how to find the length of a line segment from coordinates. Double-check each step in calculate the length of horizontal and vertical line segments to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not know how to find the length of a line segment from coordinates
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1179
(Diagram: A coordinate grid with a line segment from (0,2) to (3,1)) The line segment is one side of a square. Tom says a vertex of the square could be (2,-2) . Katie says a vertex of the square could be (1,5) . Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Both Tom and Katie. A common mistake here: Believes a coordinate cannot be placed beyond the bounds of the grid shown. Double-check each step in can calculate missing coordinates in a geometric shape to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes a coordinate cannot be placed beyond the bounds of the grid shown
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1235
What is the equation of this line? (Diagram: A coordinate grid from 0 to 10 in the y direction and -5 to 10 in the x direction. There is a line drawn passing through the coordinates: (-5,7) (0,7) (5,7) (10,7))
  • A. y=7 x
  • B. x=7 y
  • C. y=7
  • D. x=7
Explanation: The correct answer is y=7. A common mistake here: Includes the x variable when giving the equation of a horizontal line. Double-check each step in recognise the equations of horizontal lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Includes the x variable when giving the equation of a horizontal line
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1246
What is the equation of this line? (Diagram: A set of axes with a line drawn on. The lines passes through the points: (0,2) (1,2) (2,2) (3,2) and (4,2))
  • A. x=2
  • B. x=2 y
  • C. y=2 x
  • D. y=2
Explanation: The correct answer is y=2. A common mistake here: Thinks x = ? is a horizontal line through ? . Double-check each step in recognise the equations of horizontal lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Thinks x = ? is a horizontal line through ?
L1 eedi diagram eedi_1405
What is the equation of this line? (Diagram: A graph showing a vertical line going through a number on the x axis. The number is one box to the right of -10, where there are 5 boxes between 0 and -10.)
  • A. y=10
  • B. x=-9
  • C. x=-8
  • D. y=-8
Explanation: The correct answer is x=-8. A common mistake here: Assumes every dash on a scale represents an increase of 1. Double-check each step in recognise the equations of vertical lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Assumes every dash on a scale represents an increase of 1
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1425
(Diagram: A set of axes with a vertical line going through 3 on the x axis) What is the equation of this line?
  • A. x=3
  • B. y=3
  • C. x y=3
  • D. y=3 x
Explanation: The correct answer is x=3. A common mistake here: Confuses the equations of vertical and horizontal lines. Double-check each step in recognise the equations of vertical lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Confuses the equations of vertical and horizontal lines
L1 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1436
What is the equation of the x axis?
  • A. x=1
  • B. x=0
  • C. x=y
  • D. y=0
Explanation: The correct answer is y=0. A common mistake here: Thinks x = 1 at the x axis. Double-check each step in know the equation of the axes to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Thinks x = 1 at the x axis
L1 eedi diagram eedi_1443
What is the equation of this line? (Diagram: A graph showing a horizontal line going through 4 on the y axis.)
  • A. y=4
  • B. x=4 y
  • C. y=4 x
  • D. x=4
Explanation: The correct answer is y=4. A common mistake here: Believes the equation of a horizontal line is in the form x = ay, where a is the y intercept. Double-check each step in recognise the equations of horizontal lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes the equation of a horizontal line is in the form x = ay, where a is the y inte...
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1453
What is the midpoint of these two coordinates? (Diagram: A set of axes with two points (-4,5) and (6,2) plotted.)
  • A. (1,3.5)
  • B. (5,3.5)
  • C. (2,7)
  • D. (2,3)
Explanation: The correct answer is (1,3.5). A common mistake here: Believes midpoint calculation is just half of the difference. Double-check each step in calculate the midpoint between two coordinates in 2d, where all the values are integers but at least one is negative to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes midpoint calculation is just half of the difference
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1469
Points P, Q and R are equally spaced along the line segment, as shown in the sketch. The coordinates of P and R are marked on the diagram. What are the coordinates of Q ? (Diagram: A graph with 4 quadrants showing a line joining points R(2, 7), Q (unknown coordinate in 3rd quadrant) and P (-4, -10))
  • A. (-1,-1)
  • B. (-2,-3)
  • C. (-1,-2)
  • D. (-1,-1.5)
Explanation: The correct answer is (-1,-1.5). A common mistake here: Does not divide by 2 when finding midpoint. Double-check each step in calculate the midpoint between two coordinates in 2d, where all the values are integers but at least one is negative to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not divide by 2 when finding midpoint
L1 eedi diagram eedi_1533
Natalie is working out the distance between these two points. She has worked out the lengths of the triangle. What is the distance? (Diagram: The image is of two points (-7, -3) and (4,2) joined by a sloped straight line, and then a horizontal and vertical line to create a right-angled triangle. The horizontal line is marked 11 and the vertical line is marked 5.)
  • A. √(146)
  • B. 55
  • C. √(32)
  • D. √(96)
Explanation: The correct answer is √(146). A common mistake here: Does not link Pythagoras Theorem to finding distance between two points. Double-check each step in calculate the distance between two coordinates in 2d, where all the values are integers but at least one is negative to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not link Pythagoras Theorem to finding distance between two points
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1636
Tom and Katie are discussing what coordinates would complete a square. Tom says (1,8) and (5,8) Katie says (3,6) and (3,2) Who is correct? (Diagram: A pair of axes with no labels, 1st quadrant only. 2 points are marked with red dots and are labelled with co-ordinates: (1, 4) and (5, 4).)
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Both Tom and Katie. A common mistake here: Believes the sides of a square must be horizontal and vertical, not diagonal. Double-check each step in solve coordinate geometry questions involving quadrilaterals to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes the sides of a square must be horizontal and vertical, not diagonal
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1643
(Diagram: A set of axes: x-axis from -4 to 4, y-axis from -4 to 4. A red line is drawn from (-2,2) to (2,2).) The red line is _________ to the y axis.
  • A. Parallel
  • B. Opposite
  • C. Perpendicular
  • D. Equidistant
Explanation: The correct answer is Perpendicular. A common mistake here: Believes a line segment can be opposite an axis. Double-check each step in understand key loci terms like equidistant and perpendicular to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes a line segment can be opposite an axis
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_203
Mark is working out the distance between these two points. What type of triangle would help him? (Diagram: Axes with not scales drawn on. Two points are marked, (4,10) and (9,2))
  • A. Isosceles
  • B. Right-angled
  • C. Equilateral
  • D. Scalene
Explanation: The correct answer is Right-angled. A common mistake here: Does not link Pythagoras Theorem to finding distance between two points. Double-check each step in calculate the distance between two coordinates in 2d, where all the values are positive integers to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not link Pythagoras Theorem to finding distance between two points
L1 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_228
Tom and Katie are arguing about midpoints. M is the midpoint between points P and Q . Tom says if you know the location of M only, then there is more than one possible location for P and Q . Katie says if you only know the locations of M and Q , then there is more than one possible location for P . Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Tom. A common mistake here: Believes there is more than one line which passes through a given pair of points. Double-check each step in given a midpoint and a coordinate work out the position of the other coordinate, where all the values are integers but at least one is negative to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes there is more than one line which passes through a given pair of points
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_343
What shape do you get if you join up the midpoints of the sides of this shape? (Diagram: An irregular quadrilateral drawn on a grid)
  • A. Parallelogram
  • B. Rhombus
  • C. Kite
  • D. Rectangle
Explanation: The correct answer is Parallelogram. A common mistake here: Does not remember that a rhombus has equal sides. Double-check each step in identify a rectangle when presented non-conventionally to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not remember that a rhombus has equal sides
L1 eedi diagram eedi_432
This is a part of the table of values for the equation y=3 x (Diagram: A table with two rows and two columns. The top left box has 'x', with a star in the top right box. The bottom left box has 'y' with a 9 in the bottom right box.) What should replace the star?
  • A. 6
  • B. 39
  • C. 27
  • D. 3
Explanation: The correct answer is 3. A common mistake here: Thinks the inverse of subtraction is multiplication. Double-check each step in given a positive y value, find an x value in a table of values for lines in the form y = mx + c to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Thinks the inverse of subtraction is multiplication
L1 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_536
Tom and Katie are arguing about distance and coordinates. Tom says (3,-2) and (2,-3) are equidistant from the origin. Katie says (4,1) and (7,-2) are equidistant from the origin. Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Tom. A common mistake here: Believes the sum of change in x and change in y gives the distance between points. Double-check each step in compare the lengths of diagonal line segments without calculating the exact length to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes the sum of change in x and change in y gives the distance between points
L1 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_709
What is the equation of this line? (Diagram: A coordinate grid with a red line drawn on passing through the points: (-2,-10) (-2,-2) (-2, 0) (-2,4) (-2,10))
  • A. y=10
  • B. x=-1
  • C. y=-2
  • D. x=-2
Explanation: The correct answer is x=-2. A common mistake here: Counts squares instead of the correct scale when plotting coordinates. Double-check each step in recognise the equations of vertical lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Counts squares instead of the correct scale when plotting coordinates
L1 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_72
What is the equation of the y axis?
  • A. y=1
  • B. y=0
  • C. y=x
  • D. x=0
Explanation: The correct answer is x=0. A common mistake here: Thinks y =1 is the y axis. Double-check each step in know the equation of the axes to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Thinks y =1 is the y axis
L1 eedi diagram eedi_84
What is the equation of this line? (Diagram: A graph of a horizontal line going through 3 on the y axis.)
  • A. y=3 x
  • B. y=3
  • C. x=3
  • D. x=3 y
Explanation: The correct answer is y=3. A common mistake here: Includes the x variable when giving the equation of a horizontal line. Double-check each step in recognise the equations of horizontal lines from a diagram to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Includes the x variable when giving the equation of a horizontal line
L1 eedi diagram eedi_852
This is a part of the table of values for the equation y=3 x (Diagram: A table with two rows and two columns. The top left box has 'x', top right has a star, the bottom left box has 'y', bottom right has '-1'.) What should replace the star?
  • A. -3
  • B. -0.3
  • C. -1/3
  • D. There is no possible solution
Explanation: The correct answer is -1/3. A common mistake here: Mixes up the value of two terms when substituting. Double-check each step in given a positive y value, find an x value in a table of values for lines in the form y = mx + c to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Mixes up the value of two terms when substituting
L1 inline_ts le-1-1
Solve for x: 4x − 6 = 22
Explanation: Add 6 to both sides: 4x = 28. Divide by 4: x = 7.
L1 inline_ts le-1-10
Solve: 6 − x = 10
Explanation: Subtract 6: −x = 4. Multiply by −1: x = −4.
L1 inline_ts le-1-2
If 3x + 9 = 0, what is x?
Explanation: Subtract 9: 3x = −9. Divide by 3: x = −3.
L1 inline_ts le-1-3
What is x if x/4 + 3 = 7?
Explanation: Subtract 3: x/4 = 4. Multiply both sides by 4: x = 16.
L1 inline_ts le-1-4
Solve: 2(x − 3) = 10
Explanation: Divide both sides by 2: x − 3 = 5. Add 3: x = 8.
L1 inline_ts le-1-6
Solve: 2x + 5 = 13
Explanation: Subtract 5: 2x = 8. Divide by 2: x = 4.
L1 inline_ts le-1-7
If x − 8 = −3, what is x?
Explanation: Add 8 to both sides: x = −3 + 8 = 5.
L1 inline_ts le-1-8
What is x if 5x / 2 = 15?
Explanation: Multiply both sides by 2: 5x = 30. Divide by 5: x = 6.
L1 inline_ts le-1-9
Solve: 3(x + 4) = 18
Explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x + 4 = 6. Subtract 4: x = 2.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t01_q06
When you multiply a number by 4 and then subtract 7, the result is the same as if you first subtracted 7 from the same number and then multiplied by 11. What is the number?
  • A. 10
  • B. 13
  • C. 19
  • D. 23
  • E. 31
Explanation: Step 1: Translate "multiply by 4 then subtract 7" → 4n−7. Step 2: Translate "subtract 7 first then multiply by 11" → 11(n−7). Step 3: Set equal: 4n−7 = 11(n−7). Step 4: Distribute right side: 4n−7 = 11n−77. Step 5: Solve: −7+77 = 11n−4n → 70 = 7n → n = 10.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t01_q09
Which of the following is equal to ( n – 3) 2 if n = 11?
  • A. ( n + 6)( n – 6)
  • B. ( n + 5)( n – 7)
  • C. ( n + 4)( n – 8)
  • D. ( n + 3)( n – 9)
  • E. ( n + 2)( n – 10)
Explanation: Step 1: Substitute n=11: (11−3)² = 8² = 64. Step 2: This is a "value equivalence" question — we need which EXPRESSION equals 64 at n=11. Step 3: Test each choice at n=11: A: (11+6)(11−6) = 17×5 = 85 ✗ B: (11+5)(11−7) = 16×4 = 64 ✓ C: (11+4)(11−8) = 15×3 = 45 ✗ D: (11+3)(11−9) = 14×2 = 28 ✗ E: (11+2)(11−10) = 13×1 = 13 ✗. Step 4: Answer is B. Note: B is NOT algebraically eq…
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t02_q09
What is the x- intercept of a line that passes through the point (3, 4) and has a slope of 2?
  • A. –2
  • B. –1
  • C. 0
  • D. 1
  • E. 2
Explanation: Linear Equations → slope m=2 and point (3,4) → y=2x+b, so 4=6+b and b=-2 → x-intercept means y=0 → 0=2x-2 → x=1.
L2 act_master diagram act_math_t03_q06
In the following graph, n is the midpoint of . Which of the following answer choices are the coordinates of n ?
  • A. (3, k)
  • B. (3/2, 3)
  • C. (3/2, k)
  • D. (3/2, k/2)
  • E. (k/2, k/3)
Explanation: Step 1: A = (–3, 0), B = (6, k), and N is the midpoint of segment AB. Step 2: Apply the midpoint formula: N = ((x_A+x_B)/2, (y_A+y_B)/2). Step 3: x-coordinate: (–3+6)/2 = 3/2. y-coordinate: (0+k)/2 = k/2. Step 4: N = (3/2, k/2).
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t04_q07
If a + 2b = 2, what is the value of a/(b−1) + [a/(b−1)]² + [a/(b−1)]³?
  • A. –6
  • B. 8
  • C. 10
  • D. –12
  • E. 14
Explanation: Step 1: Let r = a/(b–1). Solve for r using a+2b=2: a = 2–2b = 2(1–b) = –2(b–1). Step 2: r = a/(b–1) = –2(b–1)/(b–1) = –2 (for b≠1). Step 3: The expression is r + r² + r³ with r=–2: (–2) + (–2)² + (–2)³ = –2 + 4 + (–8) = –6.
L2 act_master diagram act_math_t04_q08
On an xy- graph, three corners of a parallelogram are located at (3, 3), (4, – 4), and (–2, –1). Which of the following points could be the remaining corner?
  • A. (8, 0)
  • B. (8, –1)
  • C. (–1, 9)
  • D. (–3, 6)
  • E. (–5, 7)
Explanation: Step 1: In a parallelogram, the diagonals bisect each other — their midpoints coincide. Step 2: If vertices A, B, C, D are the parallelogram and we know three of them plus one diagonal, find the midpoint of the KNOWN diagonal and set it equal to the midpoint of the UNKNOWN diagonal. Step 3: Let the unknown vertex be (x,y). Midpoint equations: (x+known_x)/2 = midpoint_x and (y+known_y)/2 = midpoint…
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t05_q10
What is the slope of a line that includes the points (–4, 1) and (10, –6)?
  • A. 2
  • B. –2
  • C. 1/2
  • D. –1/2
  • E. –7/14
Explanation: Slope = (y₂–y₁)/(x₂–x₁) = (–6–1)/(10–(–4)) = –7/14 = –1/2.
L2 act_master diagram act_math_t06_q09
In the following figure, line a and line b are parallel and pass through the points shown. What is the equation for line b ?
  • A. K.
Explanation: Step 1: Parallel lines share the same slope. The given line y = −(5/8)x + 3 has slope m = −5/8. Step 2: Use point-slope form with the given point (x₀, y₀) and m = −5/8: y − y₀ = −(5/8)(x − x₀). Step 3: Substitute the point and simplify to slope-intercept form. Step 4: The y-intercept works out to −3, giving y = −(5/8)x − 3 = H.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t08_q10
If , with a > 0, b > 0, and c > 0, what is the value of a in terms of b and c ?
  • A. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Given proportion: a/c = b/a (or equivalent form). Step 2: Cross-multiply: a × a = b × c → a² = bc. Step 3: Take positive square root (a > 0): a = √(bc). Step 4: Answer: B. √(bc).
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t09_q06
What is the value of 4 v ( w 2 – 3 vw ) given that v = –1 and w = 4
  • A. –84
  • B. –92
  • C. –98
  • D. –104
  • E. –112
Explanation: v=–1, w=4. 4v(w²–3vw) = 4(–1)((4)²–3(–1)(4)) = –4(16+12) = –4(28) = –112.
L2 act_master diagram act_math_t09_q08
What is the slope of the line in the following graph?
  • A. 2
  • B. –2
  • C. K.
Explanation: Step 1: Identify two points on the line from the graph: the y-intercept (0, 6) is labeled; read the x-intercept from the graph (approximately (4.5, 0) = (9/2, 0)). Step 2: Slope = (y₂−y₁)/(x₂−x₁) = (0−6)/(9/2−0) = −6/(9/2) = −6×(2/9) = −12/9. Step 3: Simplify: −12/9 = −4/3. Step 4: Answer: K. −4/3.
L2 act_master diagram act_math_t10_q06
In the following figure, f is the midpoint of . Which of the following are the coordinates of f ?
  • A. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Midpoint formula: M = ((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2). Step 2: Points G=(−6,1) and H=(9,8). Step 3: x-coordinate of midpoint = (−6+9)/2 = 3/2. Step 4: y-coordinate of midpoint = (1+8)/2 = 9/2. Step 5: Midpoint = (3/2, 9/2) = D.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t10_q07
If |6 – 4 n | > 1, which of the following must be true?
  • A. K.
Explanation: Step 1: |4n − 6| > 1. Remove absolute value: two cases. Step 2: Case 1: 4n − 6 > 1 → 4n > 7 → n > 7/4. Step 3: Case 2: 4n − 6 < −1 → 4n < 5 → n < 5/4. Step 4: Union: n < 5/4 OR n > 7/4. Answer: J.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t10_q10
What is a possible value of x if ?
  • A. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Set up the radical equation from the problem. Step 2: Square both sides to eliminate the radical. Step 3: Solve the resulting equation. Step 4: Check the solution in the original equation (extraneous roots). Step 5: The valid solution is √2 = A.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t13_q09
If -9( y - 13) = 16, then y = ?
  • A. -
  • B. -
  • C. -
  • D. - E.
Explanation: Step 1: Distribute: -9y+117=16. Step 2: -9y=16-117=-101. Step 3: y=-101/(-9)=101/9 → E.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t14_q06
The expression 9( y + 3) - 2(4 y - 4) is equivalent to:
  • A. y - 1
  • B. y + 15
  • C. y + 18
  • D. y + 23
  • E. y + 35
Explanation: Step 1: Distribute 9: 9y+27. Step 2: Distribute -2: -8y+8 (note: -2×(-4)=+8). Step 3: Combine: 9y-8y=y; 27+8=35. Step 4: y+35 → E.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t14_q08
When (2 x + 4) 2 is written in the format ax 2 + bx + c , where a , b , and c are integers, what is the value of a + b - c ?
  • A. -20
  • B. 4
  • C. 20
  • D. 32
  • E. 36
Explanation: Step 1: Expand (2x+4)²=(2x)²+2(2x)(4)+4²=4x²+16x+16. Step 2: a=4, b=16, c=16. Step 3: a+b-c=4+16-16=4 → B.
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1008
Tom and Katie are discussing how to solve: 24/x=6 Tom says a correct next line of working could be: 24=6 x Katie says a correct next line of working could be: 24=x/6 Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Tom. A common mistake here: When solving an equation with x in the denominator, thinks the inverse operation is to make it the numerator on the other side. Double-check each step in solve two-step linear equations, with the variable on one side, with all positive integers to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: When solving an equation with x in the denominator, thinks the inverse operation is to...
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1009
Tom and Katie are discussing how to solve: 3 x/2+1=1.6 Tom says a correct next line of working could be: 3 x/2=0.6 Katie says a correct next line of working could be: 3 x+2=2.12 Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Tom. A common mistake here: Believes you write the carried value in the number rather than adding it to the next place value column. Double-check each step in solve three or more step linear equations, with the variable on one side, involving positive decimals to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes you write the carried value in the number rather than adding it to the next pl...

  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Tom. A common mistake here: Does not know the meaning of perpendicular. Double-check each step in recognise perpendicular lines to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not know the meaning of perpendicular

What is the gradient of this line? (Diagram: A set of axes with a graph drawn on. The graph passes through the points (0,0) and (1,2))
  • A. 0
  • B. 2 x
  • C. 2
  • D. 1
Explanation: The correct answer is 2. A common mistake here: Believes the gradient of a line is given by an intercept. Double-check each step in given a line segment, calculate the gradient where the gradient is a positive integer to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes the gradient of a line is given by an intercept

The hypotenuse of the triangle is between the coordinates (1,2) and (7,5). The base of the triangle is labelled with 6 and the perpendicular height of the triangle is labelled with 3.)
  • A. 1/2
  • B. 3
  • C. 2
  • D. 18
Explanation: The correct answer is 1/2. A common mistake here: Believes gradient is the difference between the change in x and the change in y. Double-check each step in calculate a positive fractional gradient between two coordinates where all values are positive to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes gradient is the difference between the change in x and the change in y

Find the value of x . (Diagram: A triangle with the angles labelled: 4x, 2x - 12 and 4x + 42)

  • B. x=12.6^°
  • C. x=33^°
  • D. x=21^°

⚠ Common trap: Believes angles in a triangle sum to 360 degrees

The graph shows information about the velocity, v m / s , of a parachutist t seconds after leaving a plane. Which of the following is the best estimate for the gradient of the curve when t=8? (Diagram: A graph showing time in seconds on the x axis and velocity in meters per second on the y axis. A curve is shown with a tangent to the curve drawn in at (8,51). The tangent also passes through the point (0,40))
  • A. 51
  • B. 0.72...
  • C. 1.375
  • D. 6.375

⚠ Common trap: Does not understand the difference between a value on a graph and the gradient of a graph

  • D. y=9 x+4

Which of the following is most likely to be the graph of a linear function?
  • A. ![A graph showing a curve that starts in the bottom left, moves steeply up and right, drops half way back down, then continues steeply up and right.]()
  • B. ![A graph that starts in the top left, drops steeply down as you move right, briefly levels off, then steeply up back to the same level as the starting point, forming a symmetrical U shape.]()
  • C. ![A graph showing a straight line from bottom left to top right.]()
  • D. ![A graph with two curves. One curve is in the negative x, positive y quadrant. It starts at the bottom left close to the x axis, as we move right it rises very quickly towards the y axis then levels off to travel almost parallel to the y axis. The other curve is in the negative y, positive x quadrant. It starts at the bottom left close to the y axis, as we move right it rises very quickly towards the x axis then becomes almost horizontal to travel almost parallel to the x axis. ]()
Explanation: The correct answer is ![A graph showing a straight line from bottom left to top right.](). A common mistake here: Believes linear graphs can be curved. Double-check each step in recognise a linear graph from its shape to avoid this error.

  • A. KL
  • B. EF

  • D. C D

I think of a number. I add on five and then multiply the result by four. The answer I get is 17 . Which of the following equations represents this?
  • A. n+5 × 4=17
  • B. 4 n+5=17
  • C. n^4+5=17
  • D. 4(n+5)=17
Explanation: The correct answer is 4(n+5)=17. A common mistake here: Carries out operations from left to right regardless of priority order. Double-check each step in form expressions from situations described in words to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Carries out operations from left to right regardless of priority order

  • B. n-6 ÷ 3=21
  • C. 3(n-6)=21
  • D. n/3-6=21

Jo and Paul are arguing about a first step to solve this equation: 6(m+4)=24 Jo says you can write: m+4=4 Paul says you can write: 6 m=20 Who is correct?
  • A. Only Jo
  • B. Only Paul
  • C. Both Jo and Paul
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Jo. A common mistake here: Believes you can subtract from inside brackets without expanding when solving an equation. Double-check each step in solve two-step linear equations, with the variable on one side, with all positive integers to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes you can subtract from inside brackets without expanding when solving an equation
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1665
Tom and Katie are discussing how to solve: 5 x-1/3=2 Tom says a correct next line of working could be: 5 x-1=6 Katie says a correct next line of working could be: 5 x/3=3 Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct

⚠ Common trap: Adds or subtracts from numerator first when solving an equation with a fraction
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1679
Jodie was y years old on her birthday 3 years ago. Her father was 43 years old. This year Jodie is half her father's age. Which answer does not correctly represent this scenario?
  • A. 2(y+3)=43
  • B. y+3=43+3/2
  • C. ![A bar model showing a bar for Jodie's age as y + 3 which is half the width of her father's bar. Her father's bar is shown as 2 lots of y + 3, where the first y + 3 + y total to 43.]()
  • D. ![A bar model showing a bar for Jodie's age as y + 3 which is half the width of her father's bar. Her father's bar is shown as 46 in total, split into 2 lots of 23.]()
Explanation: The correct answer is 2(y+3)=43. A common mistake here: Does not understand bar modelling in algebra. Double-check each step in write a formula to describe a situation to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not understand bar modelling in algebra

  • B. 5 r+1=7 r
  • C. 5 r+6=42 r
  • D. 5/6+r=7

  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie

  • A. x=-2
  • B. x=2
  • C. x=5
  • D. x=10
Explanation: The correct answer is x=-2. A common mistake here: Thinks they solve mx + c = a, by substituting in the value of a for x and therefore calculating ma + c. Double-check each step in given a sketch of a linear graph solve f(x) = n to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Thinks they solve mx + c = a, by substituting in the value of a for x and therefore cal...
L2 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_879
What is the gradient of this line? (Diagram: A set of axes with a graph drawn on. The graph passes through the points (0,3) and (1,1).)
  • A. -2
  • B. -1
  • C. -1.5
  • D. -0.5
Explanation: The correct answer is -2. A common mistake here: Believes the gradient of a line is given by the x intercept. Double-check each step in given a line segment, calculate the gradient where the gradient is a negative integer to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes the gradient of a line is given by the x intercept
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_883
Tom and Katie are discussing how to solve: 5 x/3-1=-2.4 Tom says a correct next line of working could be: 5 x-3=-7.2 Katie says a correct next line of working could be: 5 x/3=-3.4 Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Tom. A common mistake here: Does not understand that when multiplying both sides of an equation by an amount every term must be multiplied by the same amount. Double-check each step in solve three or more step linear equations, with the variable on one side, involving negative decimals to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not understand that when multiplying both sides of an equation by an amount every...
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_96
Tom and Katie are arguing about a first step to solve this equation: 4 v+1=9 v-3 Tom says you can write: 5 v+1=-3 Katie says you can write: 4 v+4=9 v Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Only Katie. A common mistake here: Subtracts the smallest digit from the largest digit. Double-check each step in solve linear equations with the variable appearing on both sides, with all positive integers to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Subtracts the smallest digit from the largest digit
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_986
Tom and Katie are discussing how to solve: x-6/2=10 Tom says a correct next line of working could be: x/2=16 Katie says a correct next line of working could be: x-6=5 Who is correct?
  • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct
Explanation: The correct answer is Neither is correct. A common mistake here: Adds or subtracts from numerator first when solving an equation with a fraction. Double-check each step in solve two-step linear equations, with the variable on one side, with all positive integers to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Adds or subtracts from numerator first when solving an equation with a fraction
L2 inline_ts le-2-10
The larger of two numbers is 7 more than 3 times the smaller. Their sum is 51. What is the larger number?
Explanation: Let smaller = x, larger = 3x+7. x + 3x+7 = 51 → 4x = 44 → x = 11. Larger = 3(11)+7 = 40.
L2 inline_ts le-2-2
Two numbers have a sum of 56. One number is 3 times the other. What is the smaller number?
Explanation: Let smaller = x, larger = 3x. Then x + 3x = 56 → 4x = 56 → x = 14.
L2 inline_ts le-2-3
Solve: 3(2x − 4) = 2(x + 6)
Explanation: Expand: 6x − 12 = 2x + 12. Subtract 2x: 4x − 12 = 12. Add 12: 4x = 24. Divide: x = 6.
L2 inline_ts le-2-4
If 5x − 3 = 2x + 9, what is the value of 3x?
Explanation: Subtract 2x: 3x − 3 = 9. Add 3: 3x = 12. (The question asks for 3x, not x!)
L2 inline_ts le-2-6
Solve: (x + 3) / 2 = (2x − 1) / 3
Explanation: Cross multiply: 3(x+3) = 2(2x−1) → 3x+9 = 4x−2 → x = 11.
L2 inline_ts le-2-7
A student earns $8/hr tutoring plus a $12 registration bonus. If they earned $60 total, how many hours did they tutor?
Explanation: 8h + 12 = 60 → 8h = 48 → h = 6.
L2 inline_ts le-2-8
If 0.5x + 2.5 = 7, what is x?
Explanation: Subtract 2.5: 0.5x = 4.5. Divide by 0.5 (or multiply by 2): x = 9.
L2 inline_ts le-2-9
Solve for y: 2x − 3y = 12, when x = 3
Explanation: Substitute x = 3: 6 − 3y = 12 → −3y = 6 → y = −2.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t01_q11
What is the slope of a line on a standard xy- plane that passes through the point (1, 3) and (4, –3)?
  • A. –1
  • B. 2
  • C. –2
  • D. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Label points: (x₁,y₁)=(1,3) and (x₂,y₂)=(4,−3). Step 2: Apply slope formula: m = (y₂−y₁)/(x₂−x₁). Step 3: Numerator: −3−3 = −6. Step 4: Denominator: 4−1 = 3. Step 5: Slope = −6/3 = −2.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t01_q14
If pq – 3 r = 2, what is the value of q in terms of p and r ?
  • A. 2p + 3pr
  • B. 2p – 3pr
  • C. (2 + 3r) / p
  • D. (2 – 3r) / p
  • E. (2 + 3r)p
Explanation: Step 1: Isolate the pq term: pq − 3r = 2 → add 3r to both sides → pq = 2 + 3r. Step 2: Isolate q: divide both sides by p → q = (2 + 3r) / p. Step 3: Verify: substituting back: p × [(2+3r)/p] − 3r = (2+3r) − 3r = 2 ✓.
L3 act_master diagram act_math_t02_q14
On a standard xy- graph, what is the distance between (–2, 4) and (1, –2)?
  • A. 5
  • B. E.
Explanation: Coordinate Geometry → distance between (-2,4) and (1,-2) → differences are 3 and -6 → distance = √(3²+(-6)²)=√45=3√5.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t05_q11
If 3 x + 5 y = 4, which of the following is equivalent to the expression (6 x + 10 y )(100 x + 100 y )?
  • A. 100 x + 100 y
  • B. 200 x + 200 y
  • C. 400 x + 400 y
  • D. 800 x + 800 y
  • E. 1,600 x + 1,600 y
Explanation: (6x+10y)(100x+100y) = 2(3x+5y) · 100(x+y). Since 3x+5y=4: 2(4) · 100(x+y) = 800(x+y) = 800x+800y.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t06_q12
If pq = 3, then p 3 q 4 + p 4 q 5 =
  • A. 12 q
  • B. 7 p + 9 q
  • C. 12 p + 20 q
  • D. 96 p
  • E. 108 q
Explanation: pq=3. p³q⁴ + p⁴q⁵ = p³q⁴(1 + pq) = p³q⁴(1+3) = 4p³q⁴. p³q⁴ = (pq)³·q = 27q. So 4·27q = 108q.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t07_q11
If you multiply a number by 3 and then add 40, the result is the same as if you first add 17 and then multiply by 2. What is the result if you subtract 9 from the number and then multiply by 4?
  • A. –60
  • B. –72
  • C. –84
  • D. –108
  • E. –124
Explanation: Step 1: Translate. 'Multiply by 3 then add 40' = 3n+40. 'Add 17 then multiply by 2' = 2(n+17). These equal: 3n+40=2(n+17)=2n+34. Solve: n=34-40=-6. Step 2: Subtract 9 then multiply by 4: 4(n-9)=4(-6-9)=4(-15)=-60.
L3 act_master diagram act_math_t09_q13
On an xy- graph, what is the length of a line segment drawn from (–3, 7) to (6, –5)?
  • A. 15
  • B. 16
  • C. 17
  • D. 18
  • E. 20
Explanation: Distance from (–3,7) to (6,–5): d = sqrt((6–(–3))² + (–5–7)²) = sqrt(9² + (–12)²) = sqrt(81+144) = sqrt(225) = 15.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t12_q11
Which of the following is a possible solution for x in terms of k for the equation ?
  • A. K.
Explanation: Step 1: Cross-multiply: x(x+2)=2k → x²+2x=2k → x²+2x-2k=0. Step 2: Quadratic formula: x=(-2±√(4+8k))/2=(-2±2√(1+2k))/2=-1±√(1+2k). Step 3: Positive root: x=-1+√(1+2k)=√(1+2k)-1 → K.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t12_q12
If , what is the value of a – b ?
  • A. 1
  • B. –1
  • C. 2
  • D. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Factor numerator (a²+2ab+b²)=(a+b)² and denominator (a²-b²)=(a+b)(a-b). Step 2: Simplify: (a+b)²/[(a+b)(a-b)]=(a+b)/(a-b). Step 3: Set equal: (a+b)/(a-b)=2(a+b). Step 4: Divide both sides by (a+b) [assuming a+b≠0]: 1/(a-b)=2. Step 5: a-b=1/2 → D.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t13_q13
While working on a problem on his calculator, Tex had meant to multiply a number by 3, but he accidentally divided the number by 3. Which of the following calculations could Tex then do to the result on the screen in order to obtain the result he originally wanted?
  • A. Multiply by 3
  • B. Multiply by 9
  • C. Divide by 3
  • D. Divide by 9
  • E. Add the original number
Explanation: Step 1: Wanted: n×3. Got: n/3. Step 2: To get from n/3 to n×3: multiply by 9: (n/3)×9=3n ✓. Step 3: Answer: Multiply by 9 → B.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t14_q11
What is the slope of the line represented by the equation 10 y - 16 x = 13?
  • A. -16
  • B. 10
  • C. 16
Explanation: Step 1: Solve for y: 10y=16x+13. Step 2: y=(16/10)x+13/10=(8/5)x+13/10. Step 3: Slope = 8/5 → H.
L3 act_master diagram act_math_t14_q15
When asked the price, in dollars, of his fancy calculator, Albert responded, "If you take the square root of the price, then add the price, the result is 66." What is the price, in dollars, of Albert's calculator?
  • A. 169
  • B. 144
  • C. 121
  • D. 13
  • E. 12
Explanation: Step 1: Let u=√p. Then: u+(3/8)u²=66. Step 2: Multiply by 8: 3u²+8u=528 → 3u²+8u-528=0. Step 3: Quadratic formula: u=(-8±√(64+6336))/6=(-8±80)/6. Step 4: Positive root: u=72/6=12. Step 5: p=u²=144 → G. Verify: √144+(3/8)(144)=12+54=66 ✓.
L3 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1752
Which of the following lines is perpendicular to x = -10 ?
  • A. y=7
  • B. x=-10
  • C. x=-1/10
  • D. x=1/10
Explanation: The correct answer is y=7. A common mistake here: Does not know the meaning of perpendicular. Double-check each step in identify perpendicular lines written in any other form to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Does not know the meaning of perpendicular
L3 eedi diagram eedi_197
These two lines are ... (Diagram: Two lines on a graph meeting at a right angle)
  • A. parallelogram
  • B. perpendicular
  • C. parallel
  • D. particular
Explanation: The correct answer is perpendicular. A common mistake here: Believes parallelogram is the term used to describe two lines at right angles. Double-check each step in recognise perpendicular lines to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes parallelogram is the term used to describe two lines at right angles
L3 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_375
Given that A C=3 A B , what are the coordinates of C ? (Diagram: A straight line labelled y=2x-1 is drawn on a set of axes. Points A, B and C are all on the line. Point A is (2,3) and B is (5,9).)
  • A. (14,27)
  • B. (21,36)
  • C. (11,21)
  • D. (9,18)
Explanation: The correct answer is (11,21). A common mistake here: Assumes a ratio is part to part instead of part to whole. Double-check each step in solve coordinate geometry questions involving ratio to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Assumes a ratio is part to part instead of part to whole
L3 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_77
Which of the following lines is perpendicular to y=3 x-2 ?
  • A. y=1/3 x+1
  • B. y=5-1/3 x
  • C. y=3 x+7
  • D. x=3 y-4
Explanation: The correct answer is y=5-1/3 x. A common mistake here: Believes the gradients of perpendicular lines are reciprocals of the same sign. Double-check each step in identify perpendicular lines written in the form y = mx + c to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Believes the gradients of perpendicular lines are reciprocals of the same sign
L3 inline_ts le-3-2
The width of a rectangle is 4 less than its length. If the perimeter is 48, what is the area?
Explanation: Let L = length, W = L − 4. Perimeter: 2L + 2(L−4) = 48 → 4L = 56 → L = 14, W = 10. Area = 14 × 10 = 140.
L3 inline_ts le-3-3
If 3x − 2y = 2 and y = x + 1, what is x + y?
Explanation: Substitute y = x+1: 3x − 2(x+1) = 2 → x − 2 = 2 → x = 4. Then y = 5. So x + y = 9.
L3 inline_ts le-3-5
Two cars start together and travel in opposite directions at 50 mph and 65 mph. How many hours until they are 230 miles apart?
Explanation: Combined rate = 115 mph. Time = 230 ÷ 115 = 2 hours.
L3 inline_ts le-3-6
John is 5 years older than Mary. In 3 years John will be twice Mary's age. How old is Mary now?
Explanation: J = M+5; J+3 = 2(M+3) → M+8 = 2M+6 → M = 2.
L3 inline_ts le-3-7
Solve: (x + 1)/(x − 1) = 4/3 for x ≠ 1
Explanation: Cross multiply: 3(x+1) = 4(x−1) → 3x+3 = 4x−4 → x = 7.
L3 inline_ts le-3-8
A retailer marks up cost by 30%. If the selling price is $91, what is the cost?
Explanation: 1.30 × cost = 91 → cost = 91 ÷ 1.30 = 70.
L3 inline_ts le-3-9
If 2a − 3b = 5 and a + b = 10, what is 3a − 2b?
Explanation: From a+b=10: a=10−b. Substitute: 20−5b=5→b=3, a=7. 3(7)−2(3)=15.

Linear Inequalities

core · 42 questions · linear_inequalities
L1 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t04_q05
If x 2 – x – 2 > 0, which of the following is the solution set for x ?
  • A. x > –1
  • B. x > 2
  • C. –1 < x < 2
  • D. x < –1 or x > 2
  • E. No solutions are possible.
Explanation: Step 1: The given expression (x²–x–2) > 0 (or equivalent quadratic > 0). Step 2: Factor: (x–2)(x+1) > 0. Step 3: The product is positive when BOTH factors are positive (x>2) OR BOTH are negative (x<–1). Step 4: Intersection with both simultaneously positive: x>2; simultaneously negative: x<–1. Combined: x<–1 or x>2.
L1 inline_ts li-1-2
Solve: −2x ≤ 8
Explanation: Divide by −2. FLIP the sign because dividing by a NEGATIVE: x ≥ −4.
L1 inline_ts li-1-3
Solve: x/3 + 1 < 4
Explanation: Subtract 1: x/3 < 3. Multiply by 3 (positive — no flip): x < 9.
L1 inline_ts li-1-4
Which correctly solves −4x > 20?
Explanation: Divide both sides by −4. Dividing by negative → FLIP the sign: x < −5.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t01_q07
Which of the following inequalities is represented by the following number line?
  • A. –5 < x < 5
  • B. –5 ≤ x < 5
  • C. –5 < x ≤ 5
  • D.
  • E. x
  • F. < 5
  • G. 5 ≤
  • H. x
  • I.
Explanation: Step 1: Identify the left endpoint — closed (filled) circle at −5 → x can EQUAL −5 → use ≤. Step 2: Identify the right endpoint — open (hollow) circle at 5 → x cannot EQUAL 5 → use <. Step 3: The shaded region is BETWEEN −5 and 5 (not outside). Step 4: Combine: −5 ≤ x < 5.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t05_q08
Which of the following is equivalent to –8( x – 2) < 3 x – 6?
  • A. x < 2
  • B. x > 2
  • C. x ≥ –2
  • D. x < –2
  • E. x > –2
Explanation: –8(x–2) < 3x–6. Distribute: –8x+16 < 3x–6. Add 8x: 16 < 11x–6. Add 6: 22 < 11x. Divide: 2 < x, i.e. x > 2.
L2 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t09_q09
Two values of v satisfy the equation |2 v – 13| + 6 = 9. What is the sum of these values?
  • A. 4
  • B. 7
  • C. 9
  • D. 13
  • E. 17
Explanation: |2v–13|+6=9 → |2v–13|=3. Case 1: 2v–13=3 → v=8. Case 2: 2v–13=–3 → v=5. Sum = 8+5 = 13.
L2 act_master word_problem act_math_t13_q07
Passes to the Renaissance Faire cost $9 when purchased online and $12 when purchased in person. The group sponsoring the fair would like to make at least $4,000 from sales of passes. If 240 passes were sold online, what is the minimum number of tickets that must be sold in person in order for the group to meet its goal?
  • A. 153
  • B. 154
  • C. 290
  • D. 334
  • E. 445
Explanation: Step 1: Online revenue = 240×$9=$2,160. Step 2: Remaining needed = $4,000-$2,160=$1,840. Step 3: In-person tickets needed ≥ $1,840/$12=153.33... Step 4: Minimum whole tickets = 154 (must round up to exceed goal) → B.
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_1017
Solve the inequality: -3>p-1
  • A. p<-4
  • B. p>-4
  • C. p>-2
  • D. p<-2
Explanation: The correct answer is p<-2. A common mistake here: Thinks subtracting a negative makes your answer smaller. Double-check each step in solve one-step linear inequalities in one variable where the variable appears on one side of the equation to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Thinks subtracting a negative makes your answer smaller

B is in the overlap of the two circles. C is in the -(5.3+x)/2 is great than 0 circle only. D is outside both circles.)
  • A. A
  • B. B
  • C. C
  • D. D

⚠ Common trap: Believes greater than/less than symbols include end values

  • B. t=9

  • C. 7 ≥ n
  • D. n ≥ 7
Explanation: The correct answer is 7 ≥ n. A common mistake here: When solving an equation, uses the same operation rather than the inverse. Double-check each step in solve linear inequalities in one variable where the unknown appears on boths sides of the equation to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: When solving an equation, uses the same operation rather than the inverse
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_264
Which of the following shows " x is greater than or equal to 6 "?
  • A. x>6
  • B. x ≤ 6
  • C. x<6
  • D. x ≥ 6
Explanation: The correct answer is x ≥ 6. A common mistake here: When using inequality notation, thinks "greater than/less than or equal to" symbols are instead the "greater than/less than" symbols and vice versa. Double-check each step in understand inequality notation to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: When using inequality notation, thinks "greater than/less than or equal to" symbols are...
L2 eedi symbolic_expression eedi_278
Which number line represents -3<x ≤ 1 ?
  • A. ![A number line with a hollow circle above -3 and an arrow coming out of it over the numbers -4, -5 etc and a filled in circle above 1 with an arrow coming out of it over the numbers 2, 3, 4, 5 etc.]()
  • B. ![A number line with a hollow circle above -3 connected to a filled in circle above 1.]()
  • C. ![A number line with a filled in circle above -3 and an arrow coming out of it over the numbers -4, -5 etc and a hollow circle above 1 with an arrow coming out of it over the numbers 2, 3, 4, 5 etc.]()
  • D. ![A number line with a filled in circle above -3 connected to a hollow circle above 1.]()
Explanation: The correct answer is ![A number line with a hollow circle above -3 connected to a filled in circle above 1.](). A common mistake here: Mixes up greater than and less than symbols. Double-check each step in represent a linear inequality in the form a ≤ x ≤ b (and other sign arrangements) on a numberline to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Mixes up greater than and less than symbols

⚠ Common trap: When solving an equation, uses the same operation rather than the inverse

At -1 there is an open, unfilled circle drawn, with a arrow pointing to the right, towards the higher positive numbers in the number line.]().   • A. Only Tom
  • B. Only Katie
  • C. Both Tom and Katie
  • D. Neither is correct

L2 inline_ts li-2-2
Luis has $80 saved and earns $15 per hour. He needs at least $200. What is the minimum number of full hours he must work?
Explanation: Set up: 80 + 15h ≥ 200 → 15h ≥ 120 → h ≥ 8. He needs at least 8 hours.
L2 inline_ts li-2-3
Solve: 2(3x − 1) > 4(x + 2)
Explanation: Expand: 6x − 2 > 4x + 8. Subtract 4x: 2x − 2 > 8. Add 2: 2x > 10. Divide by 2: x > 5.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t02_q13
What is the solution set for x for the inequality |2 x + 7| > 11?
  • A. 2 < x < 9
  • B. –9 < x < 2
  • C. x < 2 or x > 9
  • D. x < –2 or x > 9
  • E. x < –9 or x > 2
Explanation: Linear Inequalities → |2x+7| > 11 means 2x+7 > 11 OR 2x+7 < -11 → x > 2 OR x < -9.
L3 act_master symbolic_expression act_math_t09_q11
Which of the following inequalities is equivalent to ?
  • A. E.
Explanation: Step 1: Write the inequality as given (e.g., 2p − 3 ≤ 0 or equivalent). Step 2: Isolate p: add 3 to both sides → 2p ≤ 3. Step 3: Divide by 2 (positive, so inequality direction unchanged): p ≤ 3/2. Step 4: Answer: B. p ≤ 3/2.
L3 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1250
Use the graph to approximate the solution to x^2+2 x-3<-3 (Diagram: A set of axes with the quadratic graph y=x^2+2x-3 drawn on.)
  • A. -2<x<0
  • B. x<-2, x>0
  • C. x=-2
  • D. -3<x<1
Explanation: The correct answer is -2<x<0. A common mistake here: Selects y>a solutions instead of y<a solutions for a quadratic inequality. Double-check each step in solve quadratic inequalities from the graph of the quadratic to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Selects y>a solutions instead of y<a solutions for a quadratic inequality
L3 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1600

  • A. x<4
  • B. x<-1.4, x>1.4
  • C. x ≥ 0
  • D. -1.4<x<1.4
Explanation: The correct answer is -1.4<x<1.4. A common mistake here: Selects y>a solutions instead of y<a solutions for a quadratic inequality. Double-check each step in solve quadratic inequalities from the graph of the quadratic to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Selects y>a solutions instead of y<a solutions for a quadratic inequality
L3 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_1662
Use the graph to approximate the solution to x^2-x-6<-4 (Diagram: A set of axes with the quadratic graph y=x^2-x-6 drawn on.)
  • A. x<-1, x>2
  • B. -1<x<2
  • C. -2<x<3
  • D. x=2
Explanation: The correct answer is -1<x<2. A common mistake here: Selects y>a solutions instead of y<a solutions for a quadratic inequality. Double-check each step in solve quadratic inequalities from the graph of the quadratic to avoid this error.
⚠ Common trap: Selects y>a solutions instead of y<a solutions for a quadratic inequality
L3 eedi coordinate_graph eedi_614
Use the graph to approximate the solution to x^2<5 (Diagram: A set of axes with the quadratic graph y=x^2 drawn on.)
  • A. x<25
  • B. -2.2<x<2.2
  • C. x<-2.2, x>2.2
  • D. x ≥ 0
